feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: New primary key definition is invalid due to duplicate entries
entry.content:
Hello SAP.

I am new to datasphere and trying to figure it out.

I am trying to set up an intelligent lookup as illustrated from this link: Data Marketplace and Intelligent Lookup – Part 1 (sap.com) at around 11 min. 25. sec.

I am unable to insert SalesByCityView due to entity not having any primary keys. In the link provided, it does not seem that the instructor have used priamry keys in sales_by_city at around 3 min. 54 sec.

Then, when i am trying to change set a new key in e.g. JABS_test_addresses looks like this:

 

with the following error: new primary key definition is invalid due to duplicate entries. Tried removing all primary keys and then i am able to deploy the views or tables just fine. Though when I am trying to set a new primary key, the same error comes.

Could you please assist in, thoroughly pinpoint where the duplicate entry happens? And how can I fix it? Is it due to circular referencing between dependent objects?

Kind regards, 

Jannik

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: DPAGENT Monitor - Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Community, I have connected the Agent with Datasphere and in the monitor you can always see memory consumption, whether or not it is running dataflows.Normally it is usually at 70% and does not fall below this consumption, the machine where the agent is has 14 GB of dedicated ram.

My question is:
Is it normal that this % of use does not go down?

Can I make consumption go down if it is not being used?
When I run a dataflow it goes up from 70%, that is, it goes up to 75 or 80%

Best regards.

entry.title: Datalake Datasphere Load Time
entry.content:
Hello community,
I have a physical table loaded with the entire year 2023 and each month has an average of 25 million records.
I have a connection to the datalake in Datasphere and its respective table.
My question is with the times it takes to load the physical table from datasphere to datalake, to upload 25 or 26 million records takes almost 6 hours.

 

Runtime

DataFlow

Properties

Number of records

My question is as follows:
Are these load times normal? Since an ERP upload to Datasphere for the same year through the agent does not take that long.

Is it okay to upload to datalake via a dataflow?
Is there a more efficient alternative to uploading to datalake?

entry.title: variables in the Analytical Model and how can I implement them in my data models?
entry.content:
SAP has introduced several key updates to variables within the Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere. These updates include:

 

  • Using Standard Variables in Calculated Measures: You can now implement standard variables directly within calculated measures, making your models more flexible and dynamic.
  • Renaming Source Variables: Source variables can now be renamed to standard variables, which helps in maintaining consistency across models.
  • Data Type Checks: The system now includes enhanced data type verification, ensuring that the selected data types are appropriate for the variables used in your models.

Why These Updates Matter:

These updates are essential for improving the accuracy and consistency of your data models. By incorporating these changes, you can enhance your data analysis capabilities and reduce the risk of errors due to data type mismatches.

How to Implement the Updates:

Implementing Standard Variables in Calculated Measures:

  1. To use standard variables in your calculated measures, go to your model settings, Create a Calculated Measure and select the relevant standard variable from the dropdown list.
  2. Ensure that the variable aligns with the measure you are calculating to avoid logical inconsistencies.

Renaming Source Variables:

SAP has automatically renamed certain source variables to standard variables to ensure consistency across different models.

Verifying and Selecting Data Types:

After updating your variables, check the data types to confirm they are suitable for your model’s requirements.

This step is crucial to avoid any data type mismatch errors that could affect the performance of your models.

Conclusion: These updates significantly enhance the functionality and reliability of your analytical models in SAP Datasphere. By following the steps above, you can seamlessly integrate these changes into your workflow, ensuring that your models are both accurate and efficient.

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Variables in Analytic Model; Standard Variables in Calculated Measures
entry.content:
SAP has introduced several key updates to variables within the Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere. These updates include:

  • Using Standard Variables in Calculated Measures: You can now implement standard variables directly within calculated measures, making your models more flexible and dynamic.
  • Renaming Source Variables: Source variables can now be renamed to standard variables, which helps in maintaining consistency across models.
  • Data Type Checks: The system now includes enhanced data type verification, ensuring that the selected data types are appropriate for the variables used in your models.

Why These Updates Matter:

These updates are essential for improving the accuracy and consistency of your data models. By incorporating these changes, you can enhance your data analysis capabilities and reduce the risk of errors due to data type mismatches.

How to Implement the Updates:

Implementing Standard Variables in Calculated Measures:

  • To use standard variables in your calculated measures, go to your model settings, Create a Calculated Measure and select the relevant standard variable from the dropdown list.
  • Ensure that the variable aligns with the measure you are calculating to avoid logical inconsistencies.

 

Renaming Source Variables:

SAP has automatically renamed certain source variables to standard variables to ensure consistency across different models.

 

Verifying and Selecting Data Types:

  • After updating your variables, check the data types to confirm they are suitable for your model’s requirements.
  • This step is crucial to avoid any data type mismatch errors that could affect the performance of your models.

 

Conclusion: These updates significantly enhance the functionality and reliability of your analytical models in SAP Datasphere. By following the steps above, you can seamlessly integrate these changes into your workflow, ensuring that your models are both accurate and efficient.

SAP Datasphere 

SAP Analytics Cloud, data modeling

entry.title: DataSphere Data Builder Disappeared
entry.content:
I started the DataSphere 9 hour tutorial earlier and everything seemed to be going well.

I was able to use the Data Builder to create a model, following the instructions in the video.

All of a sudden, I get a message in my inbox saying that New Features had been added 7 minutes earlier.

Now I go to Home and the Data Builder icon is gone. Apparently, what has replaced it is Datasets, Modeler and Data Actions.

Now the tutorial is completely out of whack with DataSphere.  I can't create a graphical model anymore so that's shot.

How is this possible?  And now what do I do?  Wait on an updated tutorial to show up?

This is SO frustrating.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Replication Flows: Problem found - closed
entry.content:
Dear SAP Datasphere Community,

... obviously it is not possible to delete a question again?!

However, for those who read it already ... I found the problem:

The Replication Flow shows the technical name of a source system connection, not the descriptive name... I got confused with this and looked into the wrong system all the time 😞

Thanks anyway,

Martin

entry.title: can Datasphere create temporal joins
entry.content:
i wish to create a temporal join, similar to that i have done in HANA Studio.

can this be done in Datasphere?

See image

entry.title: Using versions with Datasphere Analytic Models in Planning for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Analytics Cloud, Planning and Datasphere experts.

I am starting to test with stories in SAP Analytics Cloud that pull data directly from Datasphere analytical models.

Since I don't create the model directly in SAC I don't know what I would have to do to be able to use different versions as well as the Planning functions of SAP Analytics Cloud.

I have checked if there is any function when creating the tables, replication flows, sql views or in the analytical model itself that allows me to add these versions for subsequent planning.

I have also tried to create a model with planning functions in SAP Analytics Cloud bringing the information from a Datasphere model, but I don't know how to do it since when selecting the source I can't choose Datasphere.

How could I solve this problem?

Thank you very much for your help

entry.title: How to change remote tables mismatched data types.
entry.content:
Hallo,

How do I change the data type for the remote table? 

I am joining the Sales Order and Sales Order Item table using the Sales Order ID. The join "Join 1" has a data type mismatch between column "SALESORDERID" and column "SALESORDERID". Please suggest is there a way to modify standard ones.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - public vs private tenants
entry.content:
Hi all,

SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere are both part of SAP BTP. With SAP Analytics Cloud, we have the option of purchasing private tenant licenses, or public tenant licenses, where system resources are shared between multiple customers using the same tenant. 

Does the same option exist for SAP Datasphere?

Thanks,

Hugh

entry.title: Datasphere performance issue
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

   I am facing some performance issues with datasphere modeling. Here is my design structures:

Background: We have to create SAC report based on COPA Actual/Plan Data and some other External Data as well.

Modeling Steps refer to above:

1. Preload COPA Actual Data(20 M Records), Budget Data(1 M Records), External Data(10 K Records) to local Table and store table data in memory. Then create views based on local tables and make projections to reduce the no. of columns. Filters and Aggregations are also applied to reduce the total no. of records.

2. Use Input parameters to limit amount of data access.

3. Create UNION for Actual and Plan data

4. Create UNION for External data and above(3)

5. Aggregation based on final data structure

6. Various Calculated column(simple calculation) created

7. Fact source view created(Measures: 110 / Attribute: 45) and used by Analytic Model. Till this point, no. of records have reduced to 2M

SAC then use this Analytic Model for Reporting

Now here comes the trouble: 

1. When I Preview data in the Analytic Model, it takes 30~40 seconds to show the result, the acceptable response time is within 10 secs.

2. While the model is consumed by SAC, as we create more calculations(YTD, Last year YTD...etc) SAC crashes.

Is there any suggestion for performance improvement ?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: How to check database connectivity from os for java system?
entry.content: r3trans -d this for ABAP systems. What is command for Java system?

entry.title: ABAP CDS extractor on NSDM_E_MCHBH (replacement of MCHBH) in not getting delta in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have created a ABAP CDS delta extractor on NSDM_E_MCHBH (replacement of MCHBH) using CDC. SAP recommends to use replacement if available. When the extractor is used in DataSphere Replication flow, the delta load is failing. 

Error - CDS view is Inconsistent and Definition of the view is too complex for automatic CDC

Note - We are getting warning "Definition of view is too complex for automatic CDS" and "Data extraction of CDS entity not possible" in ABAP perspective. We ignored the warning as it was for other extractor's as well and they are fine.

Note , even extractor on MCHBH we faced the same issue.

Please let me know how we can get delta from table MCHBH using ABAP CDS view extractor.

Thanks,

entry.title: Import/Export Route from Dev/QA/Prod
entry.content:
Hello 

Would like to know how the depoyment in Datasphere works, if there are Dev - QA - Prod Tenant. 

Dev - Export Package_A will be created 
QA - In Import Queue, we will see the corresponding Export Package_A, and deployment will be done.
Prod - What are the Options? 

Can we still use the same Export Package_A ? if yes ? how to use the same Package_A and import to Prod ? 

Manualy Export is the only option from QA to Prod ??

entry.title: system tables of DSP aren't visible in core DSP
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

Is there a way to access system tables like VIEW_COLUMNS in core SAP Data Sphere (excluding Hana Explorer)?

 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Can not use replication flows to replicate CDS Views from S4HANA OP to Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi all,
I want to replicate CDS Views from S/4HANA On Premise to Datapshere using Replication Flows but I get this error while trying to import the CDS Views "Getting dataset definition failed for <the path to cds view>"

I have checked the relevant SAP Notes but could not figure out what the problem is.
In the CDS Views, there is also these annotations specified

@Analytics:{

dataCategory: #DIMENSION,

dataExtraction: {

enabled: true,

delta.changeDataCapture: {

automatic : true

}}}

Appreciate help from everyone in the community. Thank you!

@tobias_koebler @DanielKraemer @VinayBhatt 
I have seen the articles from you guys, maybe you can help here...? Many thanks!

entry.title: Inconsistent Replication Flow Behavior in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Good day,

I am currently working on implementing business content within SAP Datasphere and have encountered issues with the replication flows. The flows are inconsistent—sometimes they run and complete successfully in a short time, while other times, the same flow hangs and takes hours to finish, despite processing a similar number of records.

I've also observed that manually restarting a specific object within the replication flow often resolves the issue, and it completes successfully. However, when the system automatically retries the process, it tends to hang. Additionally, I have scheduled the loads, but they frequently end up locking because the previous replication flow is still running.

Has anyone experienced similar issues, or does anyone have suggestions for ensuring the replication flows run more reliably? Your assistance would be greatly appreciated.

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: How to create fiscal year period hierarchy in datasphere
entry.content:
I Need to create Fiscal Year Period Hierarchy . Below is the required structure that we need to display 

Year > Quarter > Period ,Year

entry.title: FreeHand SQL query with input parameters
entry.content:
Good morning SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere experts.

I recently created a successful OData connection from a view with Datasphere parameters to use the data in an SAP Analytics Cloud Planning model.

The problem I have is that I want to select the parameters from a FreeHand query when creating the model and I get different errors.

I am testing several syntaxes:

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)/Set

And I get this error:

If I use this syntax

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP/CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)/Set

I get an error like this:

When I use this syntax:

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)

I get this error:

So I understand that the syntax here is incorrect.

I have tried ending the statement with /Results but it doesn't work either.

I have consulted other questions and documentation such as these:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-how-to-use-odata-v4-with-parameters-exposed-from-a-s-4hana-cds-view/qaq-p/12690021
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-sac-free-hand-query/qaq-p/12622748
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwplaolap/3361384392.html

But I have not been able to find a solution

What would I have to do to be able to do the freehand query for views with input parameters coming from Datasphere over OData connections?

Thank you very much!
